ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the tweet */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was posted */,
  "hashtags" TEXT /* Hashtags included in the tweet */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet received */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet received */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL to the tweet on Twitter */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the tweet (e.g., -1 to 1, where -1 is negative, 0 is neutral, 1 is positive) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location (geotag) of the tweet */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., Twitter for iPhone, Twitter Web App) */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet received */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies the tweet received */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of quote tweets the tweet received */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* Combined engagement score based on likes, retweets, and replies */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_username FOREIGN KEY ("username") REFERENCES users ("username")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "profile_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile on Twitter */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the user is following */,
  "account_created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user's account was created */,
  "profile_bio" TEXT /* Bio or description of the user's profile */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile image */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is verified */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "hashtag_text" TEXT /* Text of the hashtag */,
  "popularity_score" REAL /* Popularity score of the hashtag based on usage frequency */,
  "trend_score" REAL /* Trend score of the hashtag based on recent usage */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the hashtag (e.g., politics, technology, entertainment) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_hashtags" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hashtag */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight or significance of the hashtag in the context of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "hashtag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "replies" (
  "reply_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reply */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being replied to */,
  "replied_to_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet that was replied to (if applicable) */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the reply */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user who posted the reply */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date and time the reply was posted */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the reply received */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the reply received */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL to the reply on Twitter */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the reply */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user who posted the reply */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the reply */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reply_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_username FOREIGN KEY ("username") REFERENCES users ("username"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_replied_to_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("replied_to_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "retweets" (
  "retweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each retweet */,
  "original_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet that was retweeted */,
  "retweeting_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who retweeted the tweet */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date and time the retweet was posted */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL to the retweet on Twitter */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the retweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user who retweeted */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the retweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("retweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_original_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("original_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_retweeting_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("retweeting_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement_metrics" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement event */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that was engaged with */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who engaged with the tweet */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., like, retweet) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date and time the engagement occurred */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the engagement */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user who engaged */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the engagement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_metrics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_metrics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_role_assignments" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
